title: Rocket Lab Revenue Growth and Backlog Expansion Reset Valuation Debate
text: Following record Q1 2026 earnings and massive backlog expansion, the valuation debate around Rocket Lab has been reset. The company's backlog surged to over $2 billion in contracted revenue, a 20% quarterly increase and 108% year-over-year growth. The launch backlog now sits at more than 70 missions, a new company record. CEO Peter Beck stated demand is not one of the things to worry about, with the backlog remaining super healthy for a number of years. Commercial Electron missions in backlog are now priced around $8.5 million, up from the $5-6 million range when the vehicle first came to market. The Electron factory was designed for up to 52 launches per year, with only modest additional capital investment needed to reach that rate. CEO Peter Beck emphasized that the space industry needs more launch capacity from providers who can deliver, with operators moving fast to establish infrastructure on orbit and government customers modernizing their space capabilities.
